Appearances:
Junhai Bai (415.810.4346) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona, assisted in the Mandarin language by court certified interpreter Aidong Ni;
No other appearances.

The court did not receive notice that this matter would be contested. There is no appearance for the defendants/moving parties.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. This case is now dismissed in its entirety with prejudice. On September 17, 2025 the court sustained defendants' demurrer with thirty days leave to amend to the document filed by plaintiffs Junhai Bai and Xiaofei Li which was referred to and deemed to be their first amended complaint. Despite being informed by the court on multiple occasions, including January 14, 2026 and March 4, 2026, that their failure to file a second amended complaint would result in dismissal of this case, plaintiffs have chosen not to file a second amended complaint. This court has also repeatedly told plaintiffs that their position that they do not have to file a second amended complaint to proceed with their case due to a ruling by the Court of Appeal lacks merit. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

Tingting Qin, #567957 Court Certified Mandarin Interperter for Plaintiff's

Hearing is held. Discussed, not ARGUED. Through the assistance of the Court Certified Mandarin Interperter Tingting Qin, Court reads its tentative ruling to pro per plaintiffs. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS STEPHANIE YIP, SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend.

Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Stephanie Yip's motion to dismiss is continued to March 4, 2026 to give plaintiffs Junhai Bai and Xiaofei Li one more opportunity to file a second amended complaint. Absent good cause for their failure to do so, if plaintiffs do not file a second amended complaint by March 3, 2026, at the continued hearing the court will exercise its discretion to dismiss this case per CCP 581(f)(2) due to the failure to file an amended complaint after a demurrer was sustained with leave to amend. Based on the court's review of the document entitled "Reply About Order Sustaining Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Stephanie Yip's Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint" filed on December 5, 2025, it appears to be plaintiffs' position that they are not required to file a second amended complaint to be able to prevail in this lawsuit. Plaintiffs are mistaken. The effect of the order sustaining the demurrer to the first amended complaint with leave to amend filed September 17, 2025 is that plaintiffs do not have any operative complaint in this case unless and until they file a second amended complaint. Without an operative complaint, the plaintiffs are not able to prevail in this case.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS STEPHANIE YIP, AND SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The declaration of counsel establishes that defendants attempted to meet and confer via email to meet and confer pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 430.41 prior to filing the Demurrer to Amended Complaint on June 5, 2025. On April 22, 2025, Plaintiffs filed a request for a "direct judgment based on facts according to [the First District Court of Appeal.]" Plaintiffs further stated they would be out of the country between June and November, 2025. On April 28, 2025, Plaintiffs filed a written document explaining that it was impossible to comply with Judge Van Aken's April 17, 2025 order to re-file their First Amended Complaint because that document had been lost and no longer exists. Finally, Plaintiffs suggested in an email to defense counsel on May 24, 2025 that they would be available to meet and confer after August 18, 2025. Yet when defense counsel attempted to contact Plaintiffs by email after August 18, 2025, Plaintiffs did not respond.

The document titled "Plaintiff required to smooth out the injure and give the defendant due compensation," filed on May 20, 2025, shall be referred to herein as "First Amended Complaint," or "FAC." The FAC fails to adequately state a cause of action against defendants for the following reasons: (a) some of the allegations are vague and unclear; (b) the FAC fails to allege that the minor plaintiff is appearing through a Guardian Ad Litem; (c) the FAC fails to allege compliance with the Government Tort Claims Act; and (d) fails to adequately allege a cause of cause for intentional or negligent infliction of emotional distress. Accordingly, the demurrer is sustained with 30 days leave to amend. Defense counsel shall submit a proposed order after hearing to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Cindee F. Mayfield; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CFM)
